channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇮🇷 Trump says Iran ceasefire on "life support" as peace talks falter
item.description: President Trump rejected Iran's counteroffer to end the war, calling it "unbelievably weak" and criticizing Tehran's demands. The Strait of Hormuz remains closed as diplomatic efforts stall.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK sanctions Iran-linked network for planning attacks
item.description: Britain froze assets and imposed travel bans on individuals accused of laundering billions and plotting Iranian-backed hostile activity on UK soil.

item.title: 🇬🇱 US in secret talks to open new military bases in Greenland
item.description: The US is seeking to establish three bases in southern Greenland, according to officials familiar with the discussions, marking a significant Arctic strategy shift.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Putin signals Ukraine war "coming to an end" as EU assesses weakness
item.description: Russian President Putin's comments about the war ending come as EU's Kallas says Putin is in a "weaker position than ever before" amid continued Western support for Ukraine.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU sanctions Israeli settlers over West Bank violence
item.description: The EU's foreign policy chief announced sanctions against violent Israeli settlers, saying "extremism and violence carry consequences." Israel called the move "arbitrary."

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 Musk, Cook among 17 CEOs to join Trump on China visit
item.description: Top US executives including Elon Musk (Tesla/SpaceX), Tim Cook (Apple), and Larry Fink (BlackRock) will accompany President Trump to Beijing. The trip comes amid fragile tariff truce and Iranian war tensions affecting US-China relations.

item.title: 🇿🇦 South African president refuses to resign after impeachment ruling
item.description: Cyril Ramaphosa said he will legally challenge a report that cleared the way for parliament to consider impeachment proceedings over the Phala Phala scandal involving cash theft from his private farm.

item.title: 🇺🇸 White House press dinner shooting suspect pleads not guilty
item.description: Cole Tomas Allen, 31, pleaded not guilty to federal gun charges and attempted assassination of President Trump after allegedly firing at a Secret Service agent during the White House Correspondents' dinner.

item.title: 🇵🇭 Philippine senator flees ICC arrest over Duterte drug war role
item.description: Senator Ronald Dela Rosa has evaded a warrant related to the drug war during Duterte presidency, fleeing abroad as ICC intensifies its investigation.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK sanctions 85 people and entities over pro-Kremlin narratives
item.description: British government targets individuals and organizations accused of helping Russia forcibly deport and militarize Ukrainian children, calling it one of its "toughest measures" to date.

item.title: 🇰🇵 Russia paid North Korea up to $13.8B for war support
item.description: North Korea has earned billions for supplying weapons and soldiers to Russia's war in Ukraine, according to a new South Korean intelligence report.

item.title: ⛽🇺🇸 Trump proposes suspending federal gasoline tax amid Iran war
item.description: President Trump announced plans to suspend the federal gasoline tax to ease prices as the Iran war continues to disrupt oil markets and keep the Strait of Hormuz closed. Critics note he cannot implement this unilaterally without Congress.

item.title: 🌏🛢️ Asia braces for second wave of energy shocks from Iran war
item.description: Asian economies are preparing for another wave of energy disruptions as the Iran conflict continues, with shipping routes through the Strait of Hormuz remaining blocked and oil supplies constrained.

item.title: 🇳🇬🇹🇩 Dozens of Nigerian Fishermen Feared Dead After Chad Air Strikes on Boko Haram
item.description: A fishermen's leader reports that 40 people are feared dead after Chad conducted air strikes on Boko Haram positions near Lake Chad, either from the strikes or drowning while fleeing.

item.title: 🇦🇪🇮🇷 UAE Secretly Carrying Out Attacks on Iran, WSJ Reports
item.description: The Wall Street Journal reports that the United Arab Emirates has been conducting covert attacks against Iran amid the ongoing conflict, marking a significant escalation of Gulf state involvement in the US-Iran confrontation.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Mossad Chief Warns Against Gofman Appointment in Letter to High Court
item.description: Outgoing Mossad chief sent a letter to Israel's High Court warning of "very great risk" from the appointment of Gofman, according to Haaretz. The warning comes amid significant security leadership transitions in Israel.

item.title: ⚓ US Navy Submarine Arrives in Gibraltar as Iran Conflict Continues
item.description: A US Navy nuclear-powered submarine arrived in Gibraltar on Sunday, reinforcing Western naval presence in the Mediterranean amid ongoing tensions with Iran and operations in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: 🇨🇦🇹🇷 Canada Eyes Turkey as Defense Industry Partner, Official Says
item.description: Canada is seeking lessons from Turkey's homegrown defense industrial ecosystem, with a senior Canadian official saying Ottawa looks to Ankara as a model for developing vital defense capabilities as a mid-power ally.

item.title: 🛢️ OPEC Oil Output at 26-Year Low Amid Iran War, Reuters Survey Shows
item.description: OPEC oil production has fallen to its lowest level in 26 years as the Iran conflict disrupts regional supply chains and the Strait of Hormuz blockade continues to impact global energy markets.

item.title: 🏛️ Nazi-looted painting found in home of Dutch SS leader descendants
item.description: A portrait stolen from Jewish art collector Jacques Goudstikker during World War II was discovered in the home of descendants of Hendrik Seyffardt, a Dutch SS general, after a family member came forward to return it.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Six migrants found dead in shipping container at Texas rail yard
item.description: Six people, believed to be migrants, were found dead inside a shipping container at a Union Pacific rail yard near the Mexico border in Laredo, Texas, with heat stroke suspected as the cause of death.

item.title: 🚢 Last passengers evacuated from hantavirus-hit cruise ship as new cases confirmed
item.description: The final passengers from the MV Hondius have been evacuated to 20 countries for quarantine, with new hantavirus cases confirmed in France and the US, as the WHO says the public health risk remains low.

item.title: 🇧🇦 Bosnia high representative resigns amid US backing uncertainty
item.description: Christian Schmidt, Bosnia's international peace envoy, resigned after appearing to lose US support for his role. Schmidt had held the position since 2021.

item.title: 🇱🇧🇮🇱 Israel strikes kill at least four in southern Lebanon
item.description: Israeli attacks on southern Lebanon followed forced displacement warnings for nine towns, as the conflict between Israel and Hezbollah intensifies despite an official ceasefire.

item.title: 🇮🇱⛪ Israeli soldiers jailed for desecrating Virgin Mary statue in Lebanon
item.description: Two Israeli soldiers disciplined after one placed a cigarette in a Virgin Mary statue's mouth while the other photographed the act, drawing condemnation as an "anti-Christian" incident.
